summary: search honors its MCP read-only contract end-to-end: read-only store open + missing-store early return (search.go), search on learnHookSkipList so the pre-run initializer skips it, and the post-run journal/derive path honors the same skip list so a cold home gets zero files (root.go).
reason: Greptile review (PR #1489) found a read-only MCP command could create and migrate local SQLite state — first via search's own writable Open, then via the generated learning pre-run hook that runs before RunE.
validated_outcome: Cold GITHUB_CONTENTS_HOME: search exits 0 with empty result and creates 0 files; journaling verified still active for non-skip commands (stats writes journal-YYYYMMDD.jsonl); existing store hits unchanged.
